title: Isotrema vuquangense
taxonomy: Isotrema vuquangense is classified as a species of plant in the genus Isotrema, which is a part of the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification places Isotrema vuquangense within the order Gentianales. At the kingdom level, it is classified as a Plantae, which further categorizes it as a flowering plant. The class is Magnoliopsida, also known as dicotyledons, which indicates that the plant has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage. Its full classification is Isotrema vuquangense, Magnoliopsida, Gentianales, Apocynaceae.

intro: Isotrema vuquangense, also known as Vu Quang's snake flower or Vu Quang's snake plant, is a plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Vietnamese medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Isotrema vuquangense include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
